candidates.studentStem: A:åtg+6ha, sårb70->48, arter18->22, mål:minska följd. B:åtg+6ha, sårb saknas, arter18->22, mål:beskriv insats. C:åtg startad, sårb70->70, arter18->18, mål:löst direkt. D:åtg saknas, sårb70->48, artlista18, mål:ersätt klimat. Vilken rad har bäst stöd?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A has action plus 6 ha, vulnerability 70 to 48, species 18 to 22 and a bounded goal to reduce consequence. B lacks vulnerability, C has unchanged vulnerability and species, and D lacks action and uses only an art list. Correct A has best support. (b) Distractor types: B is Type I action-only shortcut, C is Type II label-without-effect shortcut, D is Type III consequence-without-action shortcut. (c) Cognitive dimensions: action-effect reasoning, vulnerability interpretation, species outcome and bounded conclusion. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests evaluating climate-action evidence. (e) Knowledge link map: action -> vulnerability -> species -> bounded goal. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: answer labels are neutral and data must be integrated.

shortcut_check.reviewerNote: Pure text format is allowed here because the stem contains compact scenario or numeric data; the answer requires multiple inferences.

findings: The stem can be solved from the single unique goal cue 'mål:minska följd' because the other goal phrases are weak or implausible. This violates the no-one-fact-solvability gate even though the numeric data are otherwise compact and within limits.

exactRepairTextIfFail.studentStem: A: åtg+6 ha, sårb 70->48, arter 18->22. B: åtg+6 ha, sårb saknas, arter 18->22. C: åtg startad, sårb 70->70, arter 18->18. D: åtg saknas, sårb 70->48, artlista 18. Vilken rad ger starkast stöd för effekt av åtgärden?
exactRepairTextIfFail.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A has an action of 6 ha, vulnerability 70 to 48 and species 18 to 22, so it gives the strongest evidence for an effect of the measure. B has the same action and species change but no vulnerability value, C has no outcome change, and D has lower vulnerability without an action and only a species list. (b) Distractor types: B is Type I missing-outcome shortcut, C is Type II action-label-without-effect shortcut, D is Type III outcome-without-action shortcut. (c) Cognitive dimensions: action-effect reasoning, vulnerability interpretation, species outcome interpretation, evidence completeness, bounded conclusion. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests evaluating whether action data and biological outcome data support a climate-adaptation effect. (e) Knowledge link map: 1. action of 6 ha in A -> identify an actual measure -> needed to connect outcomes to an intervention; 2. vulnerability 70 to 48 in A -> infer reduced consequence risk -> needed for the effect claim; 3. species 18 to 22 in A -> include biological outcome evidence -> needed to avoid a risk-only answer; 4. B/C/D missing or mismatched data -> reject incomplete proxies -> needed to eliminate distractors. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: unique goal wording was removed; all answer labels are neutral; the answer requires integrating action, vulnerability change, species change and distractor rejection. Can answer from text only? No. Can answer from one fact only? No.
